tenders.title: Outsourced Activity 2026 - 85100000
tenders.organization: UNIVERSITY HOSPITALS COVENTRY AND WARWICKSHIRE NHS TRUST
tenders.summary: The activity relates to carrying our surgery for long waiter patients on the waiting lists of specific surgeons in the hospital. There is insuffiicient capacity in existing NHS core hours to carry out
tenders.detailedDescription: The activity relates to carrying our surgery for long waiter patients on the waiting lists of specific surgeons in the hospital. There is insuffiicient capacity in existing NHS core hours to carry out the surgery for these patients and so we are seeking additional capacity from outside of the NHS. The activity in scope of the outsourcing relates to specific surgoens who have practising privileges to operate privately at this particular Independent Sector Provider. The advantages of ensuring the continuity of the surgeon is that the patient will not require to be reviewed as an outpatient and will not require diagnostic tests to be repeated  - this saves both time and money, and is less demanding on the patient in terms of taking time out to attend repeat additional and avoidable outpatient attendances - which in some cases can also involve radiological diagnostic investigations.

tenders.title: S30089 Future Asphalt Surface Course Linking to NH Net Zero - Phase 4
tenders.summary: This work order supports National Highways' urgent push toward net zero by safely trialling and proving new low carbon road surfacing materials before they are used more widely on the network. It focu
tenders.detailedDescription: This work order supports National Highways' urgent push toward net zero by safely trialling and proving new low carbon road surfacing materials before they are used more widely on the network. It focuses on testing innovative asphalt technologies under real traffic conditions to make sure they are durable, safe, and deliver genuine carbon and whole life cost savings compared with traditional materials. The work is important because roads are a major source of construction emissions, and without evidence from properly monitored trials, we cannot confidently update standards or roll these solutions out at scale. There is also a time critical element, as trials must align with live maintenance schemes and suitable weather windows to avoid performance or safety risks. By using a cost reimbursable contract and strong technical oversight, the project manages risk while ensuring National Highways can make informed, safe decisions that protect road users and accelerate progress toward its 2040 net zero commitment.

tenders.title: B Lock Penstocks Replacement
tenders.summary: KBS Maritime have a requirement for penstock replacements at B Lock within Portsmouth Naval Base. The project consists of 10 in number replacement penstocks in 6 chambers overall. The penstock system
tenders.detailedDescription: KBS Maritime have a requirement for penstock replacements at B Lock within Portsmouth Naval Base. The project consists of 10 in number replacement penstocks in 6 chambers overall. The penstock system controls sea water via a culvert system between the harbour, the basin and the dry docks. These include levelling penstocks of circa 8 metres deep to drainage penstocks circa 18 metres deep. The replacement programme will replace the current cast irons doors with new with specification to be agreed along with the associated shafts, wedges, door nut, brackets, platforms, ladders and actuation and road plates.  This project is due to commence in Q4 2026 with all to be replaced by Feb 2030.  Contractors must be able to demonstrate design, manufacture and installation of penstock replacements in a marine environment including all site management under CDM regulations and competency of Health and Safety compliance and safe practices. Contractors must also possess at least five years' experience working within a marine environment. Contractors who do not provide suitable and sufficient evidence of this will not be considered

tenders.title: S30075d Third Generation Midlands Regional Traffic Model Build
tenders.summary: The existing traffic models have delivered substantial efficiency savings for National Highways by streamlining traffic modelling and economic appraisal activities across Project Control Framework (PC
tenders.detailedDescription: The existing traffic models have delivered substantial efficiency savings for National Highways by streamlining traffic modelling and economic appraisal activities across Project Control Framework (PCF) schemes and have played a critical role in underpinning the RIS2 and RIS3 business cases. However, the current models are based on 2019 travel patterns and, while providing forecasts to 2061, no longer fully reflect post COVID changes in travel behaviour. This project is therefore essential to update the baseline year to 2025 and produce a refreshed set of forecasts, ensuring a robust, credible and contemporary evidence base to support decision making. Delivered as part of the Road Traffic Modelling RTM programme, the project focuses on the development of the third generation Midlands Regional Traffic Model MRTM3, replacing the existing MRTM2 model. The refreshed models will strengthen support for both existing and emerging use cases, maintain confidence in future investment decisions, and require appointed consultants to work proactively with other suppliers across the RTM3 programme to ensure consistency and coherence across the national modelling framework.

tenders.title: S30075c Third Generation South West Regional Traffic Model Build
tenders.summary: The existing traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework PCF schemes an
tenders.detailedDescription: The existing traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework PCF schemes and have been fundamental in underpinning the RIS2 and RIS3 business cases. However, the current models are based on 2019 travel patterns and, despite producing forecasts to 2061, no longer accurately reflect post COVID changes in travel behaviour. This project is therefore critical to update the baseline year to 2025 and produce a refreshed set of forecasts, ensuring the evidence base remains robust, credible and fit for purpose. Delivered as part of the Road Traffic Modelling (RTM) programme, the project focuses on the development of the third generation South West Regional Traffic Model (SWRTM3), updating the existing SWRTM2 model. The refreshed models will provide a stronger evidential foundation for existing and emerging use cases and support effective investment decision making, with appointed consultants expected to work collaboratively across the RTM3 programme to ensure consistency and coherence of the national modelling framework.

tenders.title: S30100 EV Charge Point Strategy and Delivery Plan
tenders.summary: National Highways requires a consultant to develop a comprehensive, evidence based Electric Vehicle Strategy and Delivery Plan that will enable the organisation's transition to a fully electric fleet
tenders.detailedDescription: National Highways requires a consultant to develop a comprehensive, evidence based Electric Vehicle Strategy and Delivery Plan that will enable the organisation's transition to a fully electric fleet and supporting infrastructure. This includes assessing current and future energy demand across all sites, defining technical and operational requirements for Electric Vehicle charge points, identifying and evaluating energy provision and storage solutions, engaging with stakeholders, analysing fleet usage and working practices, ensuring digital and cyber security compliance, developing specifications for national rollout, designing procurement and delivery approaches, preparing a full business case, and producing a resilient implementation programme that aligns with operational needs, grid constraints, and the organisation's net zero commitments.

tenders.title: S30081 RTM3 Mobile Phone Network Data
tenders.summary: The traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework (PCF) schemes and have
tenders.detailedDescription: The traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework (PCF) schemes and have been integral to the development of the RIS2 and RIS3 business cases. However, the current models are based on 2019 travel patterns and do not fully reflect post COVID travel behaviour. The Road Traffic Modelling 3 (RTM3) programme therefore seeks to update the models to a neutral 2025 post COVID baseline and produce refreshed forecasts, ensuring a robust and credible evidence base to support existing and emerging use cases. This project focuses on the procurement of expanded mobile phone network data to inform the development of prior matrices for RTM3, capturing detailed origin destination patterns across trip purposes, modes and user classes. The data is essential for model calibration, validation and forecasting, and consultants will be required to work proactively with those leading the Prior Matrix Technical Coordination Group to ensure consistency and coherence across the RTM3 programme.
